calibration.trend_state_question: No transitions warranted on single-cycle evidence. T6 flagged for 30-day decay audit at next /audit (28 days elapsed since last validation, May 14). T8 and T12 accumulating advance signals — multi-cycle confirmation check due at next /audit for CONTESTED consideration. T1 reading-swing risk elevated given A1 oscillation amplitude.
calibration.thesis_question: DRIFTING. Fork C cascade risk and Fork A accumulation both advancing. Fork D' lower bound deteriorating. Central thesis (materialist bargaining; no actor controls constraint surface) holds structurally. Drifting toward Fork C/Fork A dominant-strategy resolution within the thesis, not outside it.

Fork_A.rationale: CENTCOM strikes June 9-10 + Iranian GCC multi-axis response; Trump bridge/power threat; A1 hawkish posture holding 24h arc

Fork_C.rationale: 4th accident surface (Iran-GCC US-bases) confirmed; 24h A1 oscillation; structural impasse + active kinetics = cascade conditions

KEC_derived.note: [DERIVED] Fork A + Fork C + tail. For footnote use only; primitives lead.

calibration.note_6_12m_matrix: No 6/12m structural-prior update this cycle. No trend state transition, no constraint shift, no major-version increment. 6/12m matrix unchanged from v4.2.

finding_cards.target: Iranian apex access — Mojtaba/A4 functional apex channel; external-actor triangulation

finding_cards.finding: Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif letter routed through FM Araghchi June 8-9 (Iran International); no Mojtaba response or visual; no apex-level engagement with Pakistan mediation channel. A4 two-level structure holds: elected-government access channel functioning for mid-tier diplomacy; apex remains opaque to external actors.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: A4 provisional per v4-3-principal-validation-manifest.md (S2). Alternative principal: SNSC staff / Khamenei personal office / Aerospace Force command. Discriminating evidence this cycle: none. 7th+ consecutive cycle without Mojtaba visual or apex-access signal. Attribution: inherited, not validated this cycle. Principal-validation: carries forward provisional status.

Critical Threats June 1 IRGC-affiliated media inference that suspension order originated from IRGC channel (T3 analytical) — not Vahidi direct. Iran maintains 'HEU stays' posture confirmed via Araghchi FM channel June 9 (T1 direct FM statement), but attribution below the FM is opaque.
reference_trends: T1 hold
T4 hold
finding_cards.conflict_notes: A4 provisional per manifest S2. Vahidi-direct named statement is the primary discriminating evidence. 6th consecutive absence. Principal-validation: no update; provisional carries. Alternative: Khamenei personal staff retaining HEU-disposition authority directly, with Vahidi as implementer rather than decision node. Discriminating evidence: Vahidi press conference with direct attribution, or second-cluster corroboration of council structure independent of Iran International.

finding_cards.target: Iranian domestic economy — rial rate, bazaar stress, regime revenue

finding_cards.finding: USD/IRR parallel rate 1,790,000 June 11 (Bonbast), up from Day 88 trough 1,709,000 but below 20%/week threshold. No bazaar closure reports. No reported tax-revenue collapse. Depreciation trend continues but below acute-stress threshold. Bazaar structurally opaque: no T1-T2 bazaar-strike signals this cycle.
reference_trends: T3 hold
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Bazaar-closure signal remains the primary trigger criterion; absent again this cycle. Rial depreciation within tolerable range per BS-3 definition. Regime revenue: no new OFAC designations touching oil receipts this cycle.

finding_cards.target: Secondary sanctions — GL-V cascade; Chinese bank exposure; SWIFT corridor degradation
discounts_applied: Bessent letters: single-outlet claim, no Treasury confirmation; -30%
finding_cards.finding: GL-V cascade window: 18/30 days elapsed as of June 11. NFRA (Chinese banking regulator) warning to major banks on Iran exposure reported (Reuters June 9, T2). Bessent letters to 2 unnamed Chinese banks reported (Bloomberg June 10, T2 single-source — cluster provenance unverified). No major Chinese bank formally designated. No SWIFT corridor cut reported.
reference_trends: T3 hold
finding_cards.conflict_notes: GL-V trigger requires formal designation or confirmed SWIFT cut; neither confirmed this cycle. NFRA warning and Bessent letters are precursor signals, not trigger-level events. Cluster provenance on Bessent letters: Bloomberg June 10 single-source anonymous; no wire pickup confirming distinct cluster. Apply -50% to Bessent letters.

finding_cards.target: Self-defense vs resumed operations discriminator — CENTCOM strike classification
finding_cards.finding: June 8: US Apache helicopter downed by Iranian forces near Al-Tanf corridor (CENTCOM T1). June 9-10: CENTCOM struck 3 Iranian military positions in Syria/Iraq in stated self-defense response (CENTCOM T1 readout — named 'self-defense' explicitly, no new operation name). Iran GCC retaliation: ballistic/drone strikes on Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan US bases June 9-10 — all intercepted, no US casualties reported (Reuters, AP T2). Trump June 9: 'final throes of the negotiation' — hawkish/optimistic hybrid. Trump June 10: 'we will bomb the bridge and the power' — highest-amplitude hawkish posture post-ceasefire. Iran FM Araghchi June 10: 'ceasefire has become practically meaningless'; 'foreign forces should leave the region.' PROBE-7 self-defense discriminator criteria: (a) CENTCOM self-defense framing — holds; (b) reactive to named incident (Apache) — holds; (c) no new operation name — holds; (d) proportionality window (within 48h, 3 targets) — holds; (e) deal-direction non-reversing — STRAINED (Trump bridge/power threat June 10 is highest-amplitude non-deal posture; Iran FM apex hardening). Discriminator verdict: Fork C self-defense arc, not Fork A resumed operations. But criterion-e strain is significant; one more Trump escalatory statement without deal movement = potential reclassification.
finding_cards.trigger_match: Fork C cascade risk up; Fork A accumulation up; new 4th accident surface (Iran-GCC US-base strikes)
finding_cards.proposed_revision: 30d matrix: Fork C ↑ (30-39% → 32-41%); Fork A ↑ (18-28% → 20-30%). Appendix B: flag 4th accident surface for BS-16 update at next /audit. PROBE-7 criterion-e strain log for Day 106 SITREP.
reference_trends: T2 advance — multi-axis proxy doctrine demonstrated at GCC base layer
T3 hold — apex-deterrent floor not broken (no principal targeting)
T12 advance — Iranian reconstitution enabling multi-axis strike
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Principal attribution: CENTCOM strikes attributed to SecDef/CENTCOM commander chain, not Trump direct. Trump statements concurrent but not the command authority. Iranian GCC strikes: attributed to IRGC Aerospace Force (CENTCOM characterization) — consistent with A4 provisional (IRGC channel). No discriminating evidence on whether apex approved GCC expansion or Aerospace acted with delegated authority. Alternative: Aerospace Force acting with pre-delegated rules of engagement, not apex instruction — materially different for escalation-control reading.

finding_cards.target: Oil/Hormuz — Brent price, Kharg Island, commercial traffic, Lloyd's insurance
finding_cards.finding: Brent crude $93.50/bbl June 11 (Reuters). $29 backwardation holds (6-month strip). No Kharg Island incident. No commercial tanker interdiction reported. No Lloyd's Hormuz insurance suspension. Iranian naval exercise in Persian Gulf June 9 (IRGC announcement) — deterrence signaling, no commercial impact. Trigger threshold (Kharg event or Lloyd's suspension) not reached.
reference_trends: T3 hold — Hormuz deterrence functional without commercial closure
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Brent at $93.50 is within elevated-but-below-acute range per oil-price-geostrategy.md parameters. No trigger-read activation (Brent not >$90 breached to a sustained level requiring activation; intraday touch is monitoring, not activation). Iranian naval exercise: signaling consistent with Hormuz deterrence posture, not operational escalation.

finding_cards.target: Israeli internal — Knesset dissolution, coalition dynamics, Netanyahu legal exposure, unilateral timeline
finding_cards.finding: Knesset dissolution 1st reading passed 106-0 June 2 (Haaretz T1-equivalent Knesset record). 2nd and 3rd readings pending; legal window for election: September 8 - October 20, 2026. Trump 'on your own' warning to Netanyahu June 9 (Reuters T2, multiple named-source corroboration). Netanyahu coalition holding as of June 11: Ben-Gvir and Smotrich maintaining support despite Trump pressure. Lebanon operations deepening: IDF expanded ground incursion reported June 10 (Times of Israel). No new Netanyahu indictment developments this cycle.
finding_cards.trigger_match: T8 Powell shifting-power advance; A23 Netanyahu spoiler modality active-coordinated advancing; Knesset dissolution advancing
finding_cards.proposed_revision: 30d matrix: Israeli unilateral window consideration elevated given election timeline pressure. T8 advancing — flag for multi-cycle confirmation at next /audit for CONTESTED consideration. A23 spoiler: Lebanon expansion + Knesset dissolution = coordinated timeline management reading.
reference_trends: T8 advance — Knesset dissolution + Trump pressure = shifting-power dynamic accelerating
T5 hold — no new normalization movement
finding_cards.conflict_notes: A23 Netanyahu principal attribution: spoiler modality inferred from Knesset timing + Lebanon escalation + Trump friction. Alternative principal: Knesset dissolution driven by Haredi draft coalition dynamics independent of Iran strategy; Lebanon expansion by IDF operational-command with Netanyahu passive. Discriminating evidence: Netanyahu public statement explicitly linking Lebanon escalation to Iran deal timing, or IDF chief public contradiction of Netanyahu Lebanon framing. Neither arrived this cycle. Inherited, not validated.

finding_cards.target: US domestic political constraints — WPR, Congressional challenge, public opinion
finding_cards.finding: No new WPR vote or resolution this cycle. June 9-10 CENTCOM self-defense strikes proceeded without Congressional challenge (Reuters). T9 VALIDATED confirmed: Congress has not mounted sustained WPR challenge to Iran operations. Trump approval: no new polling this cycle. Republican caucus: no public dissent on Iran strikes.
reference_trends: T9 hold — Congressional non-intervention posture holds
finding_cards.conflict_notes: WPR constraint remains structurally weak as predicted. Self-defense framing insulates CENTCOM strikes from WPR exposure. No counter-hypothesis activated.

finding_cards.target: Diplomacy track — deal architecture, LOI status, Oman channel, E3/IAEA
discounts_applied: Axios May 28 deal-ready claim: single-reporter scoop, no named source; -30% on formulation detail
finding_cards.finding: Timeline reconstruction: May 28 Axios scoop (deal ready, pending Trump approval) → May 31 Trump personal edits (HEU full removal + Hormuz access clause) → June 1 Iran suspended negotiations in response → June 7-8 Iran-Israel kinetic exchange → June 9 Iran rejected current proposal in full; Oman presented counteroffer (Reuters T2) → June 10 Iran FM Araghchi: 'ceasefire has become practically meaningless.' Oman channel technically alive (counteroffer tabled) but structural impasse deepened: Iran LOI ceiling (no full HEU removal, no Hormuz clause) directly contradicts Trump May 31 edits. No E3/IAEA engagement this cycle.
finding_cards.trigger_match: Fork D' lower bound pushed down; structural impasse on LOI architecture deepening; Fork B combined declining
finding_cards.proposed_revision: 30d matrix: Fork D' ↓ (21-29% → 17-25%); Fork B combined ↓ (15-24% → 11-19%). Oman counteroffer keeps track alive in theory but impasse on core terms is structural, not tactical.
reference_trends: T4 hold — Oman track alive but degraded; not collapsed
T1 hold — private-information gap on Trump's actual red lines widening (HEU+Hormuz edits vs. Araghchi 'near-signed' prior read)
finding_cards.conflict_notes: A2 provisional per v4-3-principal-validation-manifest.md (S1). Netanyahu-relay claim that Trump committed to 'full dismantlement / all HEU removed' now partially confirmed by Trump May 31 personal edits — but these are edits to a proposal, not a signed commitment. A2 discriminating evidence: not yet met (no White House readout using the exact language). May 31 edits move A2 from 'unconfirmed relay' toward 'partially consistent' but do not resolve the provisional status. Source: Axios single-reporter; -30% on formulation detail. Alternative: Trump edits were opening-position bargaining, not actual red line — would mean A2 mechanism mischaracterized. Discriminating evidence needed: Trump public statement or named NSC source confirming these as non-negotiable terms.

finding_cards.target: A1 Trump principal state — oscillation pattern, decision-moment read, hawkish vs. deal posture
discounts_applied: Trump statements: near-zero without tape action per CLAUDE.md; scored for oscillation-pattern signal, not commitment signal
finding_cards.finding: June 9: Trump 'final throes of the negotiation' (optimistic-deal framing concurrent with Apache incident) → June 10: Trump 'we will bomb the bridge and the power' (highest-amplitude hawkish posture post-ceasefire). 24-hour arc is highest-amplitude A1 oscillation recorded since ceasefire. Iran FM Araghchi June 10: 'ceasefire practically meaningless'; 'foreign forces should leave region' (T1 direct). This cycle's A1 state at close: hawkish (bridge/power threat). Oscillation pattern: 8th documented oscillation arc since Day 88; no observable attractor toward either deal-close or resumed-ops.
finding_cards.trigger_match: Fork A accumulation up; Fork D' declining; A1 oscillation at peak amplitude
finding_cards.proposed_revision: 30d matrix: Fork A ↑; Fork D' ↓ per PROBE-12'. SITREP Section 3 to characterize bridge/power threat vs. 'final throes' arc as primary A1 datum this cycle.
reference_trends: T1 hold — private-info gap driving oscillation; consistent with Fearon incomplete-information framework
finding_cards.conflict_notes: A1 principal attribution: Trump public statements near-zero informational value on commitment. Oscillation pattern is informative; individual statement is not. Alternative: bridge/power threat was negotiating pressure, not actual targeting directive — consistent with prior pattern (Day 88 'bombing campaign' threat preceded ceasefire). Discriminating evidence: SecDef/CENTCOM targeting orders, not statements. Iran FM Araghchi hardening (T1) is the higher-quality datum; suggests Iranian apex read Trump June 10 posture as unserious or pressure-tactic — 'practically meaningless' is a verdict on the negotiating architecture, not panic.

finding_cards.target: Iranian reconstitution speed — T12 state; missile retention, underground storage, import channels
discounts_applied: Israeli IC data on reconstitution: -50% single-cluster; used as one signal
finding_cards.finding: 70% of pre-conflict missile inventory retained (ISW June 8 assessment, T3). 90% underground storage operational (IISS June 9 brief, T3). 30 of 33 Hormuz targeting sites restored (War Zone June 10, T3 defense-operational). Solid-fuel imports from China confirmed via Kpler tanker tracking (T4 — Kpler is authoritative data). June 9 GCC multi-axis strike (Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan) demonstrates operational reach across 3 separate base clusters — confirmation of reconstitution hypothesis. T12 reconstitution-speed amplifier: ADVANCING multi-cycle.
finding_cards.trigger_match: T12 advance multi-cycle; BS-13 IDF-strike-degradation-overestimate visibility up ~5pp
finding_cards.proposed_revision: T12 advancing 2nd cycle with 2+ independent source clusters (ISW + IISS + operational demonstration). Flag for CONTESTED consideration at next /audit. BS-13 visibility: update from ~60% to ~65% at next audit.
reference_trends: T12 advance — reconstitution faster than IDF initial degradation assessment; multi-cycle multi-source
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Israeli IC reconstitution data: -50% single cluster applied. ISW + IISS are independent T3 clusters (different institutional sourcing). Kpler tanker data is T4 but independent (trade data, not intelligence). 3+ independent signals this cycle — strongest T12 evidence base to date. GCC multi-axis strike is operational demonstration, not inference.

finding_cards.target: Dispositional asymmetry — Weisiger framework; Iranian hardening vs. US deal-willingness
finding_cards.finding: Trump bridge/power threat = highest-amplitude hawkish posture (US side). Iran FM 'ceasefire practically meaningless' + 'foreign forces should leave' = apex maximalism on ceasefire architecture (Iranian side). Weisiger dispositional asymmetry: both principals hardening simultaneously in public posture. Structural condition for miscalculation cascade at peak level this cycle. Neither side's public posture credibly signals deal-close willingness; private channel (Oman) technically alive but LOI impasse is structural.
finding_cards.trigger_match: T8 advance — shifting-power dynamic at maximum observable tension
finding_cards.proposed_revision: Flag T8 for CONTESTED consideration at next /audit alongside PROBE-9 advance signal. Weisiger asymmetry at peak is a leading indicator for Fork C escalation, not a direct trigger.
reference_trends: T8 advance — dispositional asymmetry peak consistent with Powell shifting-power race condition
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Weisiger dispositional asymmetry reads public posture, not private. Oman counteroffer June 9 is private-channel signal in opposite direction (Iran still engaged). Public = domestic-audience management; private = actual bargaining. Do not read public hardening as private-track collapse. Distinguish audience-cost signaling from revealed preference.

finding_cards.target: Accident surfaces — new escalation pathways beyond existing BS-15 architecture
finding_cards.finding: 4th accident surface confirmed this cycle: Iranian GCC-linked ballistic/drone strikes on US bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan (June 9-10) — all intercepted, no US casualties. Prior Day 102 synthesis recognized 3 accident surfaces: (1) Iran-US direct exchange, (2) Iran-Israel exchange, (3) proxy/third-party incidents. GCC base strikes are a 4th surface previously not architecturally distinguished: Iran attacking US bases hosted by non-belligerent Gulf states. This surface has distinct escalation logic: GCC states bear collateral-risk and may trigger their own response calculus (Article 5 equivalent logic via US defense commitments to host nations).
finding_cards.trigger_match: BS-15 architecture expansion; Fork C cascade risk elevated
finding_cards.proposed_revision: BS-15 finding card to list 4 accident surfaces at next /audit. SITREP to note GCC-base surface as new Day 105 recognition. Fork C ↑ per PROBE-7.
reference_trends: T2 advance — multi-axis proxy doctrine now demonstrated at GCC base layer
finding_cards.conflict_notes: All GCC base strikes intercepted this cycle — no casualties. Demonstrates capability and willingness, not yet kinetic consequence. Escalation pathway: if one GCC-base strike penetrates intercept, host-nation political calculus changes immediately. UAE/Bahrain have US defense agreements — this surface has host-nation actors beyond the primary bilateral frame.

T6 siloviki-fragmentation trend holds VALIDATED. 28-day decay audit threshold approaching: last full T6 re-validation was May 14; June 11 = 28 days elapsed (threshold is 30 days per framework decay clause). Flag for next sweep or audit: T6 re-verify required by June 14 at latest.
reference_trends: T6 hold — but decay threshold approaching; flag for Day 106-107 re-validation
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Auto-upshift rationale: Day 102 SITREP flagged BS-9.3 April count as unresolved — this constitutes external signal touching probe target per auto-upshift rule. Running PROBE-17 as D this cycle. BS-9.3 now resolved for April+May: NOT triggered. Return to M cadence next cycle absent new BS-9 signal.

discounts_applied: UAE ambassador quote: single-outlet T3-tier regional outlet; -20%
finding_cards.finding: UAE ambassador to US called publicly for 'conclusive outcome' to Iran conflict and did not oppose ground-invasion framing (Gulf News June 10, T3 regional). MBZ-pathway: no joint troika brake-appeal this cycle; UAE moving toward tolerate-escalation posture. Saudi (MBS): quiet — no statement endorsing or opposing escalation. Qatar: Oman parallel track; maintaining mediator posture. BS-18 CRITICAL reading: MBZ-pathway decay advancing; troika brake-capacity eroding. Gulf states absorbing GCC base strikes without formal protest to Iran.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: UAE ambassador quote: single T3-outlet; -20% applied. Principal attribution: MBZ decision vs. ambassador freelancing. Discriminating evidence: MBZ direct statement or MBZ-channel communication to US administration. Not arrived. Inherited, not validated. Alternative: UAE ambassador statement is pre-authorized MBZ signaling to US administration via public channel — standard Gulf communication modality. If so, T1-quality signal in T3 packaging.

finding_cards.target: Paine death-ground framework — P-DG, P-DG2, P-AIM, P-INFO signals
finding_cards.finding: P-DG (death-ground trigger: US targeting of named Iranian principals): NOT fired. No named Iranian principal targeted June 9-10 — CENTCOM strikes hit military positions, not individual commanders. P-DG2 (terms hardening post-strike): CANDIDATE — Iran FM 'ceasefire practically meaningless' + 'foreign forces should leave' post-June 9-10 strikes meets directional criterion but is FM-channel, not apex hardening. Flag, not fire. P-AIM (aim-class): LIMITED AIMS maintained — no Iranian demand for regime change of GCC states, no maximalist territorial demand; HEU retention + Hormuz access = limited aims within Paine framework. P-INFO (accurate-advisor removal): No purge signal this cycle. Oman track alive = information channel preserved. Paine death-ground: not triggered this cycle.
reference_trends: T3 hold — apex-deterrent floor not broken
finding_cards.conflict_notes: PROBE-21 fire-bar note: 0 fires in 11+ cycles of partials. P-DG requires principal targeting; P-DG2 requires apex hardening post-strike (FM channel is below apex per two-level structure). Bar may be correctly set or may be unreachable at current targeting posture. Flag for /audit threshold recalibration per sweep cadence note.
